title: Describing the Scene
learningGoal: The learner will compose sentences demonstrating strict dichotomization between animate genitives (name + 's) and inanimate partitives (noun + of + noun), alongside correct demonstrative pronoun pluralization.
instructions: Look at the people and things around you. Write 3 sentences. One sentence must show a person owning something (using 'S). One sentence must describe a piece of an object (using OF). One sentence must point at many things far away (using THOSE).

contentItems.text: CRITICAL RULE: Never write 'The bag of Amit'. Write 'Amit's bag'. Never write 'The car's door'. Write 'The door of the car'.

writingPrompt: Write 3 sentences. Sentence 1: A person owning an object (Name + 's). Sentence 2: A part of an object (The [part] of the [object]). Sentence 3: Pointing at many things far away (Those are...).
promptGoal: Test strict execution of the animate/inanimate ownership dichotomy and plural-distal demonstrative morphology.
referenceHints: Sentence 2 (Object Part): The [Piece] of the [Whole Object] is [Color/Size].

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: This is Rahul's phone. The door of the house is blue. Those are tall trees.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: दिस इज़ राहुल'स फोन। द डोर ऑफ द हाउस इज़ ब्लू। दोज़ आर टॉल ट्रीज़।
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: यह राहुल का फोन है। घर का दरवाज़ा नीला है। वे ऊंचे पेड़ हैं।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect. Correctly employed the animate enclitic (Rahul's), the inanimate partitive (door of the house), and the distal plural (Those + trees).

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: This is the phone of Rahul. The house's door is blue. That are tall trees.
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: दिस इज़ द फोन ऑफ राहुल। द हाउस'स डोर इज़ ब्लू। दैट आर टॉल ट्रीज़।
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Critical failure. Inverted the animate/inanimate rules (phone of Rahul / house's door) and mismatched a singular demonstrative with a plural noun (That are trees).

evaluationCriteria.description: Did the user correctly attach 's to a human/animal owner? Penalize 'noun of person'.
Did the user correctly use 'of' for object parts? Penalize 'object's part'.
Did the user use 'Those' with a plural noun and the plural copula 'are'?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: That is Sarah's book. The tire of the car is black. Those are my friends.
acceptableAlternates: Here is Amit's dog. The glass of the window is clean. Those are birds.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Zero tolerance for 'That are' or 'Those is'. Strong penalty for 'The book of John'.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You wrote perfect sentences! You showed deep understanding of how to separate human ownership from object parts.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please review what you wrote. Human ownership takes an 'S (Mom's car). Object parts take OF (The wheels of the car). Do not mix them up!
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good start! Double check your pointing words. 'Those' ALWAYS means more than one thing. You must say 'Those ARE dogs', never 'Those IS dog'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Write three sentences. One about a person's item. One about a piece of an object. One pointing at many things.

hintText: Read your sentences aloud! 'The screen of the phone'. 'Ravi's phone'. 'Those are phones'. If you say 'That are', something is broken!
